title: SACRAMENTO, CA

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "AB 554 prevents health care plans and insurance companies from requiring prior authorization or step therapy for all antiretroviral drugs, including injectable medications, used for HIV/AIDS prevention.",
    "AB 554 requires lifesaving drugs to be covered without cost sharing or utilization review for individuals with private insurance.",
    "Injectable PrEP is effective for HIV prevention.",
    "Commercial plans are only required to cover formulations of PrEP with Grade A or B recommendations from the United States Preventive Services Task Force (USPSTF).",
    "Current insurance coverage does not account for existing or future formulations of PrEP that the FDA approves, which may better promote adherence and reduce HIV risk.",
    "AB 554 addresses the disproportionate impacts and higher rates of HIV transmission among ethnically or racially marginalized Californians due to limited healthcare access and awareness.",
    "In 2022, Black/African Americans represented 14% of PrEP users, while Latinx individuals represented 17% compared to white individuals, who represented 64% of PrEP users.",
    "The Latinx community comprised 57% of the total HIV diagnoses in California for 2022.",
    "Small, local clinics often cannot receive full reimbursement for injectable PrEP medications from private insurance plans, limiting overall access.",
    "Injectable PrEP is revolutionizing HIV prevention.",
    "Providers face hurdles in administering injectable PrEP, leading to delays for patient initiation.",
    "High costs and logistical challenges of purchasing and billing for injectable PrEP impact community-based providers specializing in LGBTQ+ care and HIV treatment.",
    "Equipping providers to bill under the pharmacy benefit can make injectable PrEP access more sustainable.",
    "AB 554 aims to empower healthcare providers and expand access to PrEP.",
    "The legislation removes reimbursement barriers that hinder providers from delivering all forms of PrEP, including long-acting injectables.",
    "AB 554 strengthens the fight against HIV by ensuring uninterrupted access to PrEP for everyone, regardless of income.",
    "California has strong laws ensuring patients can access PrEP without financial barriers or delays.",
    "Long-acting injectable PrEP has tremendous potential to help prevent HIV infections, especially among communities that face barriers to healthcare."
  ]
}
```
